title: trifolium angustifolium subsp. dichroanthum
intro: Trifolium angustifolium subsp. dichroanthum, also known as narrow-leaved clover or purple clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Trifolium angustifolium subsp. dichroanthum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes.
